title: The World Government’s Shadow
description: On pages 13–15, this part follows Unknown person #13, King Harald, Luffy, Unknown person #14, Usopp, Unknown person #15 as The story examines the bloodline hatred and political violence surrounding Elbaf while the danger around Loki and the God’s Knights grows.
scene: The story examines the bloodline hatred and political violence surrounding Elbaf while the danger around Loki and the God’s Knights grows.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Ever... forgive a giant who does evil!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: DAD... I'm gonna quit the marines.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: I've made up my mind, IDA...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: I don't care about appearances anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I want you to get along when I'm gone...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and do what you can to support Elbaf.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But my superiors have ordered me to not let you land!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: High ranking Marines and leaders of the world government...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I ask that you please hear me out!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Allow me to atone for the sins of the past thousand years...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I apologize for every wrong the giant race has committed!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The children of Elbaf are being raised to live without harming others...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...so that one day they may walk hand-in-hand with humanity. I do all this to prove our unwavering commitment to peace!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'll do whatever it takes to stone for our sins...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #15
dialogue.text: Even if it means becoming a slave!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Is there lightat the end of this tunnel?

summary: This part advances The Weight of Elbaf’s Bloodline through 39 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on the immediate conflict, character reactions, and the larger Elbaf mystery.
